error.sensor_swap_detected: It looks like you are replacing a sensor. Revert this change and use the 'Heating Analytics: Replace Sensor' service call instead — this is the only way to preserve the learned history for the removed sensor.
error.weather_entity_required: Please select a weather provider to continue.
error.weather_entity_not_found: The selected weather provider is no longer available. Please select another one.
error.weather_missing_temperature: This weather provider does not supply current temperature data. Please choose a different provider or connect a dedicated outdoor temperature sensor.
error.weather_missing_wind_speed: This weather provider does not supply current wind speed data. Please choose a different provider or connect a dedicated wind sensor.
error.mpc_entry_required: A Heat Pump MPC instance must be selected when Track C is enabled.

user.title: The Basics
user.description: Connect your energy meters and weather provider. This is all that is required to get started.

data_description.name: A name for this integration instance.
data_description.weather_entity: Used for forecasts, wind data, and as a fallback temperature source.
data_description.energy_sensors: All sensors measuring energy consumption (kWh) for your heating devices.
data_description.outdoor_temp_sensor: Recommended. A local sensor improves accuracy. If left blank, the weather provider's current temperature is used as a fallback.
data_description.ghi_sensor: Optional. A local pyranometer or scraped weather-station value reporting Global Horizontal Irradiance in W/m². Logged per hour and surfaced in the ghi_signal_agreement diagnostic to validate the cloud_coverage-based solar pipeline against an independent measurement. No model effect until evidence justifies pipeline integration.

physics.title: Building Physics
physics.description: Configure the physical characteristics of your home. The defaults work well for most standard houses.

data_description.wind_unit: The unit for wind speed settings on the next page.
data_description.balance_point: The outdoor temperature at which your home stops needing heat. Typically 15-17°C for well-insulated homes, up to 18°C for older buildings.
data_description.thermal_inertia: How many hours of outdoor temperature history the model considers. Use a low value for lightweight wood-frame structures, higher for heavy concrete or stone buildings.

advanced.title: Advanced Options
advanced.description: Fine-tune the learning model and enable optional features. Features that require additional input will be configured on the next page.

data_description.daily_learning_mode: When enabled, the model learns once per day at midnight (Track B) instead of every hour (Track A). Only recommended for homes with very high thermal mass (concrete/stone) or heat pumps with a high minimum modulation level. Significantly slows down the learning curve — full model characterisation may take an entire heating season.
data_description.overnight_load_shift_correction: WARNING: Only enable if you actively pre-heat or pre-cool across the midnight boundary (e.g. cheap-rate overnight charging). This corrects the daily energy total for heat stored in the building structure before midnight. If you do not do deliberate overnight load shifting, leave this OFF — enabling it incorrectly will skew the learned model. Requires Daily Learning Mode.
data_description.track_c_enabled: Enables the Track C Midnight Sync engine. Instead of using raw electrical consumption as the learning baseline, Heating Analytics fetches actual delivered thermal energy from the MPC and redistributes (smears) it across 24 hours proportionally to weather-driven heat loss. This eliminates the feedback loop caused by overnight load shifting. Requires Daily Learning Mode to be active and a connected heatpump_mpc integration.
data_description.aux_affected_entities: Which heating units are supplemented by auxiliary heat sources (e.g. a wood stove). Defaults to all units if left empty.
data_description.enable_lifetime_tracking: Maintain a cumulative history of total energy usage. Increases storage usage over time.
data_description.screen_south: Check if your south-facing windows have external motorised screens that respond to the screen-correction slider. Uncheck for facades without screens — that direction's solar gain then ignores the slider.
data_description.screen_east: Check if your east-facing windows have external motorised screens that respond to the screen-correction slider.
data_description.screen_west: Check if your west-facing windows have external motorised screens that respond to the screen-correction slider.
data_description.screen_affected_entities: Which heating units are in zones that have external screens. Units in zones without screens are treated as screen-independent — their solar coefficients are not attenuated by the screen-correction slider. Defaults to all units if left empty.
data_description.solar_affected_entities: Which energy sensors actually have their consumption reduced by sun in the room. Check sensors (a) in rooms with sun exposure AND (b) where heating is controlled by air temperature. Uncheck floor-heating cables driven by a slab/floor-temperature thermostat (the cable runs to maintain slab setpoint regardless of sun in the room above) and sensors with no solar exposure (basement sensors, wine cellar, garage, parasitic loads). Excluded sensors get a zero solar coefficient and skip every solar learning path. Energy sensors added after initial config default to checked — uncheck if the new sensor doesn't respond to solar.
data_description.use_dedicated_wind_sensor: Override weather entity wind data with a dedicated sensor. Not recommended — local sensors near buildings are affected by turbulence and shielding, producing systematically low readings. Weather entity wind data is preferred.
data_description.secondary_weather_entity: Optional secondary provider for long-range forecasts (Shadow Forecasting). Useful for validating a new weather source before switching.
data_description.forecast_crossover_day: Number of days to use the primary weather source before switching to the secondary for longer-range predictions.
data_description.solar_hotspot_attenuation_gamma: Experimental opt-in. Dimensionless multiplicative attenuation (1 − γ) applied to predicted solar gain for screened-facade units when the sun is high (elevation > 30°) and screens are actively deployed (slider < 80%). Compensates for residual hotspot/sky-light loss not captured by the per-direction screen transmittance. Leave at 0.0 (default, no behavioural change). Empirical envelope from field measurements puts useful values in the 0.2–0.4 range. Only applies to entities whose zone has at least one screened facade.
data_description.solar_redistribution_alpha: Experimental opt-in. Fraction of the per-unit predicted solar gain that is temporally redistributed from the originator hour to the prior 3 hours (exponential-decay kernel). Applies only to low-elevation (sun < 30°) eligible hours where the empirical battery-tail effect was observed. Energy is conserved — this is redistribution, not amplification or attenuation. Leave at 0.0 (default, no behavioural change). Empirical envelope from field measurements puts useful values in the 0.4–0.6 range.
data_description.solar_redistribution_tau_hours: Time constant (hours) of the exponential-decay kernel used by the tail redistribution. Larger τ spreads more weight to older lags; smaller τ concentrates weight on the most recent hour. Default 2.0 h gives a balanced 3-hour tail. Only active when α > 0.
data_description.csv_auto_logging: Write detailed hourly and daily logs to CSV files for external analysis.
data_description.max_energy_delta: Hourly readings above this value (kWh) are ignored to prevent sensor glitches from corrupting the model.
data_description.hourly_log_retention_days: How many days of hourly-resolution data to keep. Longer retention enables cross-seasonal retraining and deeper diagnostics. 90 days is conservative (recommended for Raspberry Pi); 365 days uses approximately 9 MB of storage. Reducing this value permanently deletes hourly data beyond the new limit — daily summaries are not affected.

feature_config.title: Feature Settings
feature_config.description: Configure the features you enabled on the previous page.

data_description.indoor_temp_sensor: Optional. Measures indoor temperature changes to calculate how much energy is stored in the building structure overnight.
data_description.thermal_mass_kwh_per_degree: Compensates for heat stored in the building structure during indoor temperature changes. Rule of thumb: (Floor_m² × 35) / 1000 / avg_COP. Example: 250 m² house with COP 3.0 ≈ 2.9 kWh/°C. Leave at 0.0 to disable.
data_description.mpc_entry_id: Select the Heat Pump MPC integration instance to read thermal data from. Required when multiple MPC instances are installed.
data_description.mpc_managed_sensor: Which energy sensor is managed by the MPC. Its meter data is time-shifted by load scheduling and will use synthetic baseline instead of direct readings.
data_description.wind_speed_sensor: Dedicated wind speed sensor. Not recommended for most installations.
data_description.wind_gust_factor: Controls the impact of wind gusts on heat loss calculations. 0.0 ignores gusts entirely; 1.0 applies them fully. Older or draughty buildings often benefit from a higher value.
data_description.wind_threshold: Outdoor wind speed above which the high-wind correction model is applied.
data_description.extreme_wind_threshold: Outdoor wind speed above which the extreme-wind correction model is applied.
data_description.csv_hourly_path: File path for the hourly CSV log.
data_description.csv_daily_path: File path for the daily CSV log.

reconfigure.title: Edit: The Basics
reconfigure.description: Update your primary sensors and weather provider.

data_description.name: A name for this integration instance.
data_description.weather_entity: Used for forecasts, wind data, and as a fallback temperature source.
data_description.energy_sensors: Add new heaters or permanently remove old ones. Do not use this field to replace a renamed or broken sensor — removing and re-adding a sensor destroys its learned history. Use the 'Heating Analytics: Replace Sensor' service call for that instead.
data_description.outdoor_temp_sensor: Recommended. A local sensor improves accuracy. If left blank, the weather provider's current temperature is used as a fallback.
data_description.ghi_sensor: Optional. A local pyranometer or scraped weather-station value reporting Global Horizontal Irradiance in W/m². Logged per hour and surfaced in the ghi_signal_agreement diagnostic to validate the cloud_coverage-based solar pipeline against an independent measurement. No model effect until evidence justifies pipeline integration.
